tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated blood and urine creatinine, significant proteinuria, and very high urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio, indicating severe renal dysfunction. Urine is abnormal in appearance, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, suggesting impaired renal concentrating ability and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, excluding chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are consistent with acute or advanced chronic kidney disease; urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
